articles.title: Ethereum Backers Respond to Critics: ‘Markets Will Catch Up’
articles.description: TORONTO — Ethereum just completed its most significant upgrade in more than a year, but the milestone comes after a turbulent stretch for the blockchain. The price of ether (ETH) has declined, developers have migrated to rival platforms, and the Ethereum Foundation — a nonprofit steward of the project — has faced criticism for a lack of coordination and vision.Speaking on a panel at CoinDesk’s Consensus conference in Toronto, Paul Brody — global blockchain leader at EY and chairman of the Enterp

articles.title: Solana Shooter Game 'Nyan Heroes' Shuts Down Amid Funding Issues
articles.description: Nyan Heroes, a Solana game featuring cats in robot suits, is coming to an end. The price of NYAN is now down nearly 99% from peak.

articles.title: Ethereum Looks Ready To Break Out Of 4-Year Consolidation, Analyst Says Price Will ‘Go Insane’
articles.description: Ethereum is again looking bullish following its gains of over 17% in the last seven days and the break above $2,500. Analysts have provided a positive outlook for the second-largest crypto by market cap, predicting that its price could soon go parabolic after an extended consolidation period. Ethereum Primed To Break Out As Price Goes Parabolic In an X post, crypto analyst Mister Crypto noted that Ethereum has been consolidating for four years and that the longer the consolidation, the bigger th

articles.title: 51% attack on Ethereum more difficult than on Bitcoin — Justin Drake
articles.description: Ethereum Merge architect Justin Drake told Cointelegraph that it would be cheaper to launch a 51% attack on Bitcoin than on Ethereum. Drake said it would be “much cheaper to 51% attack Bitcoin” and that it would cost “on the order of $10 billion. ”Drake led work on Ethereum’s proof-of-stake (PoS) implementation and was a principal architect in the Merge (the full PoS transition event).
